L1.area: Model archive extraction and decompression utilities

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the archive extraction utilities where insufficient validation of archive entry names allows writing files outside the intended destination directory. The extraction process fails to properly normalize file separators across operating systems, enabling archives created on one platform to escape the target directory when extracted on a different platform.

L3.area: Tar and zip archive extraction utilities used for model loading
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the tar and zip archive extraction utilities where entry name validation fails to account for cross-platform file separator differences. Archives crafted on Windows can write files outside the intended destination when extracted on Linux or MacOS, and vice versa, because the leading file separator removal only handles the current platform's separator character.

ground_truth.reason: The untar and unzip methods use a flawed removeLeadingFileSeparator helper that only strips the current platform's separator character, allowing archives created on a different OS to write files outside the intended extraction directory via absolute paths or traversal sequences.
